hook: AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked for help with one decision.
caption:
AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked for help with one decision.

I just needed to know whether to fire a contractor.

Instead I got: a framework for evaluating vendor relationships, a section on communication best practices, a note about 'considering both perspectives,' and — I'm not joking — a reminder to 'consult a legal professional.'

Nobody asked.

The problem isn't that AI is dumb. It's that AI doesn't know YOU.

It doesn't know that you've already thought about both perspectives for three weeks.

It doesn't know you need a blunt answer, not a buffered one.

It doesn't know you make decisions fast and regret the slow ones.

So it defaults to: cover everything, offend nobody, help no one.

Your BootFile changes that. It tells the AI upfront — this person thinks fast, wants directness, hates disclaimers, and already did the emotional processing. Just help them move.

Same AI. Completely different conversation.

That's the whole idea.
